description: Cross-language seed-time normalization for port verification (ct-subsystem-extraction.md §6.2). Applied at seed time to BOTH the extracted-spec side (S) and the re-implementation side (S') before functor discovery, so a TS spec and a Python port are compared through a language-fair, coarser profile dial. All transforms are compare-time: the persisted hom-profile / edge artifacts stay maximal-precision (the established contract); only the in-memory search inputs are reshaped. Held lightly (MetaCoding-63v) — the marginals are DATA-DERIVED per side at run time, not frozen here; this file carries only the STRUCTURAL choices (which kinds fold, which idioms fold).

reweight.note: §6.2.1 — each profile dimension is divided by its per-SIDE edge-kind marginal frequency before cross-language cosine, so language-dense kinds (TS TYPE_OF/RETURNS_TYPE density; Python IMPLEMENTS scarcity) stop dominating the seed. Marginals are computed from each side's own collapsed edge distribution at run time; `floor` bounds the divisor for absent kinds. This is the §7.2 candidate fix, adopted as the default for any cross-language comparison.

idiom_shims.note: §6.2.3 — per-language symbol-level folds applied before role quotienting. A folded member is merged into its CONTAINS parent (its edges reroute to the parent; the member leaves the object set). Matched against the member's qualified_name. Kept short and explicit; adding a target language is a table edit, not code.

ts.reason: property getter/setter pairs fold into their field/owner (TS accessor idiom)
interface-only declaration files fold into their implementations

py.reason: dunder protocol methods fold into their class role (Python data-model idiom)
leading-underscore private helpers fold into their owner (Python privacy idiom)
